reviews.review_msg: It is currently my last semester at SFU and I am so grateful that I was able to enroll in Amin's BUS 465 course as a final goodbye to SFU. Along with being the most understanding professor, he is very knowledgeable and one of the most technical professors I have met. Overall 10/10 professor!! Thank you, Amin!

Course content is a bit technical but he only tests you on the basics stuff, lab assignments are so easy it takes 5 minutes to complete, and he's easy on marking assignments and exams. One of the most enjoyable group project since he let's you make any web app you want, and teaches you basic HTML, CSS, JS, PHP, MySQL, etc.

I took 2 classes with Amin and learned a lot of useful skills from both! He is laid back and grades fairly, but averages tend to be super high. Regardless, easy A if you do above average. His exams can get more technical because his background is CMPT so bonus if you have previous experience. Overall he's one of my favorite profs at SFU :D

Amin is very personable, however he should not be teaching in the business faculty. He did not differentiate the marks at the end of the semester, and literally gave everyone the same grades on all the assignments and projects regardless of individual effort. He skips over important concepts in lecture, and can be condensending.
Amin is a very nice guy and he wants you to succeed. You get to learn a lot of useful material throughout the term. Prepare for power assignments in advance and do some practice exams before final.

Eloquent speaker that really gets you thinking. Super respectful prof who encourages questions and gives plenty of feedback to any comments/concerns raised in class. Summarizes key concepts well in each lecture. Exams are fair. Questions are based off of the lecture readings.

Sit down and buckle up! This is a crash course on JavaScript, CSS, HTML, PHP, and MySQL geared towards business students with no formal training. Amin did a great job of squeezing this rigorous curriculum altogether into a fast-paced learning environment. If you like a challenge and you're great at problem solving, this is the prof for you.

Wouldn't say it's easy, the course is designed to be technical, but this Prof is super nice with help if you ask for it. Not only he's helpful, but he understands the pains of students and keeps on trying to find ways to alleviate them. The prof has a cmpt and data background, so don't expect your usual business prof.

Very nice and an easy marker. Final is very fair and he always tries to help students as much as possible. Interesting to see things from his computer science background point of view as well as business.

Although he is new but Amin is very knowledgeable to the subject. You will find the course is heavy on weekly power assignments and quizzes but Amin will try to answer your questions and his gradings are pretty easy.
